name: Classical Nahuatl
notes: Classical Nahuatl (Nāhuatlahtōlli), the Uto-Aztecan (Nahuan) literary language of the Aztec empire and colonial central Mexico, recorded in the traditional Hispanic-based ('Jesuit') orthography. The grapheme system is identical to the Central Nahuatl base (nhn): ⟨c⟩ = /k/ but /s/ before ⟨e i⟩; ⟨qu⟩ /k/, ⟨cu⟩ /kʷ/, ⟨z⟩ /s/, ⟨x⟩ /ʃ/, ⟨ch⟩ /t͡ʃ/, ⟨tz⟩ /t͡s/, ⟨tl⟩ /t͡ɬ/, ⟨hu⟩ /w/, ⟨ll⟩ /lː/, ⟨h⟩ the saltillo /ʔ/; vowels ⟨a e i o u⟩. As in the base, phonemic VOWEL LENGTH is not written in this orthography and is therefore under-generated relative to length-marked gold.

sources.author: Andrews, J. Richard
